items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Doctor Huayu, have you found him?

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: Nope. Been everywhere he would show up. Didn't find him. Just like you.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: Uncle Hong is getting older and has become more unpredictable. It's hard to pin him down, even on a normal day.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: Let's keep looking. We can't afford to have the Refinery Tower offline for too long.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: A travelling salesman? The mine is closed for business today. Please come back another day.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: Jinzhou is not far from here. You can take some rest there.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: You should stop rushing before the guest even says anything. Let {Male=him;Female=her} finish.

options.text: The Ministry of Development sent me to deliver these samples.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: Oh, a guest from the Ministry. Let me introduce myself. I'm the doctor of the Mine, Huayu.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: The tall guy next to me is Lei'li, the foreman here, and this is Modee, a mineral expert from the Academy.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: A few days ago, I heard about the sample agreement between the Mine and the Ministry. Please, how should I address you?

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: Ah, {PlayerName}, I see. But in the photo attached to the Ministry's request, the person in charge is a young, nervous-looking girl. How come...

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: I got the message and the photo from Uncle Hong, the senior officer-in-chief here. Look, this is the photo.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: You take out the photo from Huayu. It's clearly not you, so all you can do is show him the ID you were given as proof.

options.text: Like I said, this person isn't me.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: Hm, the ID is indeed from the Ministry of Development. Let's overlook the minor details, then. To be blunt, the Mine is facing some issues fulfilling the Ministry's request.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: Haha, this thing is legitimate. Maybe there was some little accidents back at the Ministry. Now I'll be straight: the Mine is facing some problem too, regarding the Ministry's request.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: Modee has more details.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: The Refinery Tower at the center of the Mine is offline, but some minerals require it for processing.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: So do the Ministry's samples. Right now, we're working on a solution to reboot the Refinery Tower.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: Incidents like this have happened before, but our on-site engineer was always quick to fix it and get things back on track.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: But he injured himself a while back while working on some other equipment. He's in Jinzhou recovering now.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: That left Uncle Hong to take over. He used to be in charge of the Refinery Tower when he was younger, so he's more than qualified.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: Whenever something went wrong in the past, Uncle Hong was always the one on the front lines, fixing things and coordinating repairs.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: But this time, Uncle Hong hasn't shown up, and we can't seem to find him anywhere in the Mine.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: With his absence, the Mine is having all kinds of difficulties dealing with the Refinery Tower.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: If you ask me, I think the old man's just taking it easy. Maintenance work like that has got to wear on his old bones.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Maybe he's hiding somewhere, watching us, muttering, "Damn kids, can't you let me have a little peace and quiet?"

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: I propose we all go back to our places and carry on with our jobs. The Mine has to keep functioning even with the Refinery Tower offline.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: He'll show once he gets his rest.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: Clues... The miners said they saw Uncle Hong around the transport lorries. We are sending more people to check if he's still there.

options.text: Let me be of some help.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: More people mean greater possibility. Thank you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The graph here says, “The reboot of the Refinery Tower takes a certain amount of time. First, secure a sufficient power supply during the rebooting...”

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Stop, stop. English, please. Whatever, something about power supply, right? I know who to turn to. Follow me.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: These lazy new guys. I told them to hand in the work log before their shifts ended.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Pft, in one ear and out the other. Now who's going to clean up the mess if there's an accident?

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Oh, {PlayerName}. What brings you here? And Huaxi?

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Did Doctor Huayu send you as peacemakers? Did he find out about my fight with Modee?

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: What are you talking about, Uncle Lei'li? My brother asked me to work with {PlayerName} to reboot the Refinery Tower.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Refinery Tower reboot? So you found Uncle Hong?

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: You tell Lei'li about the journal and Uncle Hong's memories.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Ah, so that's what's going on. Uncle Hong is always so sharp and on top of things. It's hard to imagine him being anything less.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: I work alongside him all the time, and I never noticed. Alright, what do you need help with?

options.text: About the power supply of the Mine.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Yes, rebooting the Refinery Tower requires sufficient power. Uncle Lei'li, you know the best about the power equipment. What's your advice?

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Well, it's not as hard as you might imagine. We have a double circuit power system at the bottom of the Mine. Either one can sustain its normal functioning.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: However, the two circuits can't run in parallel. If we force them, it could cause the breakers around the Mine to trip.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: What we can do is bring in extra external power and shut down some internal systems to focus the energy on the Refinery Tower.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Alright, I'll handle shutting down the systems. The backup mobile power sources are there at the base.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: You'll need to install them at the Refinery Tower. I'll let you know when everything's ready, okay?

options.text: Can you tell me about that fight you mentioned?

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Ugh, it's nothing big. Modee and I argue all the time. I'm used to it.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: I just can't stand his attitude. Everything he does, it's all some fantasy in his head.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: He's always going around the Mine, bragging about his grand philosophy of coexisting with Tacet Discords.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: He says it's a key evolutionary skill to coexist with new life forms, that we should consider developing alongside Tacet Discords.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: He can think about it all he wants, but just thinking won't get us anywhere. Honestly, he's just a dreamer, locked up in his ivory tower, completely out of touch with reality.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Every time I say something, he gets all worked up.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Forget it. Just talking about it is making my blood boil. Let's focus on the Refinery Tower.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: {PlayerName}, is the additional power set up?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Yes. All ready and set. What about your part?

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Just one more step. The shutdown switches come in pairs, so I need you to turn off the other one at the same time.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: It's working. The devices are being shut down. The power supply for the Refinery Tower should be sufficient now.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Thanks for your help. If everything's good here, I'll get back to my duties.

items.speaker: Lei'li
items.text: Got a whole lot of things to do. Please carry on with the good work with the Refinery Tower.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: ...Hm, so it is...

options.text: What, are you reading earlier journal entries?
You shouldn't read someone's journal without permission.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: I'm not snooping! I just thought of a really smart way to find Grandpa Hong.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: I read a story once about a detective who didn't talk much, and his friend, a doctor, helped him solve all sorts of mysteries.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: The detective would sometimes look at the bad guy's past to find clues.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: After all, a person's past usually tells you why they do things now!

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: That's why I think though focusing on today's entry is definitely the right direction, earlier entries are still important resources.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Hee hee. Now you know!

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Most of his entries are pretty short. He just sums up his day and how he felt in a few sentences.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: But I did find some longer, more detailed entries. I think they're important, like this one.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: What's your take? Doesn't this story feel weird?

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: I feel the same. Plus, the lower half of the page is left blank.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: True. It's like the story doesn’t really go anywhere, and the rest of the page is just blank.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Either way, this story feels like it's only half done.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: You know, it starts by saying it's a dream being recorded. Could that be why it feels this way?

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Right. Let's move on to the next step of the instructions.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: The graph indicates that we have to reconfigure the Refinery Tower with the console.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Let's find someone to talk to first.

items.speaker: Console
items.text: <<<Initializing system

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Big {Male=brother;Female=sister}, is this what we should do?

items.speaker: Console
items.text: <<<System initialized. Verification required...

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: A verification is required...

options.text: Let's see if the journal has any hints.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: The entry is mostly about an argument with a researcher. No information about a passcode.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: If nothing else works, maybe we'll just have to try guessing...
You'd better hold off on that. If you fail the verification too many times, the console will lock, and the system will shut down automatically.
If that happens, we'll only waste more time in the rebooting.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: Just follow the steps to unlock the console and everything should go smoothly. Good to see you again, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: I heard from a co-worker about what you're up to, and I thought you might find difficulties in passing the verification, so I came.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: Looks like I'm right on time.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: You, you... Aren't you that researcher who was fighting with Grandpa Hong, in the journal?

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: A fight? The journal? Haha, may I see the journal myself?

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Modee takes the journal and starts reading.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: This feels like what would happen between me and Lei'li, but that researcher isn't me.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: Uncle Hong is like a mentor to me, so I would never have an argument like that with him.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: In fact, I have always respected him because he taught me an important lesson.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: In science, it's not just about cold, hard numbers. There's also room for imagination.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: Like when we come across infinity in our research. To some scholars, that infinity symbol might look like radio waves moving through space.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: It was even shown in a Jinzhou sci-fi play not long ago, symbolizing a twist in the dimensions of life.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: Haha. It's just an example that just crossed my mind. You can have a look yourself at the Jinzhou Theater if you're interested.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: The play was about a catastrophe. Humanity had to send their home planet on a journey through space. It used the symbol to hint at the connection between digital life and reality.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: A single symbol can be used in different ways to create colorful worlds. Might be a bit hard to grasp now, though.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: But so many truths come from these wild, imaginative ideas. Just thinking about it gets me all fired up.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: But Lei'li? He just responds with "That's all nonsense" and completely ignores anything that seems unlikely.

options.text: The question about the coexistence with TD?

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: So you've heard about it? Yes. Even if the probability is as small as 0.0001%, it still makes sense to me.

options.text: Is this what you call a researcher's sensitivity to digits?
Is this what you call a researcher's obstinacy?

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: Hahaha, you do know a lot, {PlayerName}. Now let's get on with the issue with the Refinery Tower.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: The console's verification is done by line drawing. When you start the verification process, random images will be generated on a grid.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: You have to put your hand on the grid, and the system will draw a line to connect the coordinates you touch. Repeated coordinates will not be recorded.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: Only when the order and the direction of the line you draw is right, the verification is passed. All right, let's have a try.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: You're here. {PlayerName}, this is the map you asked for.

options.text: Mark the places in the journal on the map.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: You quickly mark the places mentioned in the journal on the map.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: For an old man, he sure gets around! But looks like it's mostly just three different areas.

options.text: Draw a circle with each of these regions as the center.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: Ah yes! A triangulation approach.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: By drawing three circles with the regions as centers, the overlapping area is where Hong is most likely to be.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: That really narrows things down. Great idea!

options.text: Just a little titbit from the Rover.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: No idea what you two are talking about, but you sure sound smart. You're pretty cool, {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: The overlapping area is a small corner in the upper living quarter. Let's go take a look!

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: Oh, by the way, you can hand me the samples now, {PlayerName}. When the reboot is finished, we will start the procedures.

items.speaker: Modee
items.text: However, it can't be done today, as the reboot can take some time.

items.speaker: Bistro Owner
items.text: Bye, Uncle Hong. Take care when you go back, eh?

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Okay, okay. Tastes just like the old days, but I just can't help feeling empty.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: What was I going to do?

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Look, {PlayerName}. That's Grandpa Hong.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: We finally found you, Grandpa Hong. Everyone's been looking for you.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: You are... Huaxi. Why look for me?

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Great. Grandpa Hong remembers me.

items.speaker: Bistro Owner
items.text: What are you talking about? I think you lost me.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Huaxi and you tell the boss what happened in a low voice.

items.speaker: Bistro Owner
items.text: Ah, that's why. I thought Uncle Hong came here after the maintenance work of the Refinery Tower, as usual.

items.speaker: Bistro Owner
items.text: Let me tell you, my cooking skills were passed down from my grandfather's generation.

items.speaker: Bistro Owner
items.text: Back then, every time Uncle Hong finished work, he'd come here for a warm bowl of Jinzhou Stew.

items.speaker: Bistro Owner
items.text: My grandfather always said that food shortens the distance from home, and also brings people closer together.

items.speaker: Bistro Owner
items.text: Jinzhou Stew isn't just a simple dish. It's a bond that connects people.

items.speaker: Bistro Owner
items.text: And for Uncle Hong, it's probably even more meaningful, since the people sitting with him here have changed since his day.

items.speaker: Bistro Owner
items.text: He's the only one left.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Eating alone always feels a bit lonely. No wonder Grandpa Hong seems so down.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Uncle Hong takes the journal. Despite initial resistance, he begins to flip through the pages eagerly.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Journal. That's it. I feel much settled now.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Grandpa Hong...

options.text: Maybe we shouldn't ask too much now.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Right. Grandpa Hong, we found your lost journal, and fixed the reboot issue of the Refinery Tower.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Anyway, the guest from the Ministry is here with the samples like they promised. That's this {Male=mister;Female=lady} beside me.

options.text: Do you have any recollection of that?

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Yes... I do. Everything feels familiar again with the presence of this journal. I'm sorry for the fuss.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: Grandpa Hong still looks a bit strange. Let's take him to my brother.

items.speaker: Huaxi
items.text: It's kind of late now, {PlayerName}. Like Uncle Modee said, the samples can't be tested as of now. Why don't you come here another day?
